findings.evidence: Mathlib/GroupTheory/Index.lean:57 defines `Subgroup.index` as `Nat.card (G ⧸ H)` and line 348 has `index_eq_card : H.index = Nat.card (G ⧸ H)` with no `[Finite G]`; the proof at lines 164-167 does not use the finite instance.
findings.fix: Remove `[Finite G]` from the theorem; the existing proof uses `AddSubgroup.index_eq_card`/`Subgroup.index_toAddSubgroup`, which are available without finiteness.
findings.issue: `card_elementaryTwoQuotient_eq_index_square` assumes `[Finite G]`, but the statement is just the definition of subgroup index after identifying the two subgroups and does not need `G` finite.

findings.evidence: TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:186-189 proves `card_elementaryTwoQuotient_eq_two_pow_twoRank` from `[Module.Finite (ZMod 2) (ElementaryTwoQuotient G)]`; Mathlib/FieldTheory/Finiteness.lean:97 `Module.natCard_eq_pow_finrank` has the same finite-module hypothesis.
findings.fix: Move this theorem before the finite-class-group section, or give it an explicit `[Module.Finite (ZMod 2) (ElementaryTwoQuotient R)]` hypothesis instead of requiring `[Finite (ClassGroup R)]`.
findings.issue: `ClassGroup.card_elementaryTwoQuotient_eq_two_pow_twoRank` is placed under `variable [Finite (ClassGroup R)]`, but the inherited theorem only needs `[Module.Finite (ZMod 2) (ElementaryTwoQuotient R)]`, matching the hypothesis already used by `twoRank`.

summary: The general construction is mostly at the right level, but two cardinality statements retain finite-group hypotheses where nearby APIs prove the stated result under weaker hypotheses.
